title: Machine Learning Concepts, Data and Evaluation
description: Machine learning paradigms, the train/validation/test split, overfitting and underfitting, and evaluating classifiers with the confusion matrix, accuracy, precision, recall and F1 score.
objectives: Distinguish supervised, unsupervised and reinforcement learning by the kind of feedback each uses
Explain why a model must be evaluated on held-out data it never trained on, and what the training/validation/test split is for
Distinguish overfitting from underfitting and describe the bias-variance tradeoff at a conceptual level
Construct a confusion matrix from predictions and compute accuracy, precision, recall and F1 score from it
Judge when precision, recall or F1 is more informative than accuracy, especially for imbalanced data

lessons.title: Learning from data: paradigms and the train/validation/test split
Overfitting, underfitting and the bias-variance tradeoff
Evaluating classifiers: confusion matrix, accuracy, precision, recall and F1
